trip.name: Philippines 2026

flights.notes: Return flight. Check connections through Seoul or Tokyo.

budget.note: Spending budget only — flights and accommodation already paid before trip.

trip.vibe: Chill romantic — mostly home, occasional outings. Cook together, explore when it feels right.

days.title: Departure Day
activities: Long flight — bring laptop, download shows

days.title: Arrival Day
activities: Grab to Makati (~30-45 min depending on traffic)
Light dinner nearby — let Christel pick
days.notes: Don't overplan. First night = settle in.

days.title: Grocery Run + Home Dinner
activities: Morning: sleep in, adjust to timezone
Grocery run with Christel — SM Makati or Landmark
days.notes: Christel wants to cook at home. Let her lead the grocery list.

days.title: Makati Exploration Day
activities: Walk Ayala Center / Greenbelt — shops + restaurants
days.notes: Greenbelt is the nice outdoor mall area. Good for a chill date.

days.title: Jollibee Day 🐝
activities: Jollibee — get the Chickenjoy, Jolly Spaghetti, and Burger Steak
Visit Poblacion — artsy/nightlife area (daytime is chill)
days.notes: Jollibee is mandatory. Order for two. Try the Peach Mango Pie.

days.title: Chill Home Day
activities: Afternoon: relax, read, nap, video games
Evening: grocery run for tomorrow's cooking
days.notes: Not every day needs to be an outing. Home days are the best days.

days.title: BGC Day 1
activities: Grab to BGC (~15 min from Makati)
Lunch at a BGC restaurant (Terra 22 or Toyo Eatery)
Walk The Mind Museum area — art installations
Home for dinner or eat in BGC
days.notes: BGC is the cleanest, most walkable area in Metro Manila. Good for a date day.

days.title: BGC Day 2
activities: Return to BGC for anything missed
Venice Grand Canal Mall (boat ride + restaurants)
Rooftop drink at a BGC bar (Z Hostel Roofdeck or similar)
days.notes: Venice Mall has gondola-style boats — touristy but fun for couples.

days.title: Home Day + Date Night
activities: Evening: dress up for date night
Dinner at Blackbird Makati (romantic, art deco, fancy)
days.notes: Blackbird is in the old Nielsons Tower. Elegant but not stuffy.

days.title: Cooking Day
activities: Big grocery run — cook something ambitious together
Try a Filipino recipe (adobo, sinigang, or kare-kare)
Evening: movie or walk around Legaspi Park
days.notes: Cooking together = best date activity. Legaspi Park has a Sunday market too.

days.title: Relaxation Day
activities: Maybe walk to a nearby café
days.notes: This is the beauty of staying in Makati. You're not rushing.

days.title: Legaspi Market + Chill
activities: Legaspi Weekend Market (if Sunday) — local food, crafts, live music
Or: Salcedo Market (Saturday) — same vibe, different area
Dinner out — try Toyo Eatery or Manam
days.notes: Both markets are walking distance in Makati. Weekend only.

days.title: Last Full Day
activities: Final grocery trip — grab pasalubong (gifts) to bring home
days.notes: Pasalubong ideas: dried mangoes, ube jam, polvoron, local coffee

days.title: Departure Day
days.notes: Allow 2+ hours for NAIA. Traffic can be brutal.

checklist.item: PH visa check (US passport = 30 days visa-free)
Exchange USD → PHP (don't do at airport)

mustTry.name: Jollibee
mustTry.order: Chickenjoy, Jolly Spaghetti, Burger Steak, Peach Mango Pie

nearby: Legazpi Sunday Market (street food + live music)

parks.name: Ayala Triangle Gardens
parks.note: Green space in the middle of Makati

packing.item: Phone charger + adapter (Type A/B, same as US)

tips: Sept is rainy season — bring umbrella, mornings are usually clear
Grab is the Uber of PH. Use it for everything.
Makati is very walkable for a Manila city. Greenbelt area especially.
ATMs charge 250 PHP ($4.50) per withdrawal. Withdraw large amounts.
7-Eleven everywhere. Cheap meals, phone top-up, ATMs.
Christel's home base is Mindanao but she'll be in Makati with you.
Cooking at home saves money AND is more romantic than eating out every night.
Jollibee delivery via GrabFood if you don't want to go out.
